title: GPSeq reveals the radial organization of chromatin in the cell nucleus
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"u7kgp","text":"With the exception of lamina-associated domains, the radial organization of chromatin in mammalian cells remains largely unexplored. Here, we describe genomic loci positioning by sequencing (GPSeq), a genome-wide method for inferring distances to the nuclear lamina all along the nuclear radius that works by gradual enzymatic restriction of chromatin from the nuclear lamina towards the nucleus center, followed by sequencing of the generated cut sites. Using GPSeq, we mapped the radial organization of the human genome at 100 kb resolution, which revealed radial patterns of genomic and epigenomic features, gene expression, as well as A/B subcompartments. By combining radial information with chromosome contact frequencies measured by Hi-C, we substantially improved the accuracy of whole-genome structure modeling. Finally, we charted the radial topography of DNA double-strand breaks, germline variants and cancer mutations, and found that they have distinctive radial arrangements in A/B subcompartments. We conclude that GPSeq can reveal fundamental aspects of genome architecture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eaeom","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"xdl61","text":"In eukaryotic cells, the genome is spatially organized and its three-dimentional (3D) architecture is vital to the proper execution of its functions1. One key feature of the 3D genome is that individual chromosomes are non-randomly positioned with respect to the nuclear periphery2–9. The nuclear lamina is thought to be the key organizer of the radial arrangement of chromatin in interphase nuclei10, by creating a large nuclear compartment where the majority of inactive chromatin clusters in the form of lamina-associated domains (LADs)11–13. Specialized sub-chromosomal regions, such as centromeres and telomeres, as well as nucleolar organizing regions, are also non-randomly positioned in the nucleus14–18. The latter contain ribosomal RNA gene clusters that coalesce to form the core of the largest nuclear body, the nucleolus, and organize chromatin in and around them19. Furthermore, inter-chromosomal interactions around the nucleolus and nuclear speckles have also been implicated in shaping the 3D genome20.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":148,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":280,"length":5},{"style":"sup","offset":398,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":539,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":706,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":876,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":1016,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dqwu","text":"        The preferential radial location of certain genomic loci in the nucleus has been variably attributed to gene density3,5,6, GC-content21–23, as well as chromosome size4,7,8,24. Additionally, transcriptional activity has also been implicated in radial nuclear organization, although it is still unclear whether transcription influences radiality or vice versa25–35. Overall, the role of genomic and epigenomic features in shaping radiality remains to be quantified, despite several attempts to model the contribution of various factors35–37. In particular, it is unclear whether the nucleus merely consists of a peripheral transcriptionally inactive compartment as opposed to a central transcriptionally active one, or whether a finer stratification exists. In this context, a major obstacle until now has been the lack of dedicated genome-wide methods to specifically tackle this aspect of chromatin organization at high resolution. To overcome this limitation, here we develop a method that allows inferring radial locations genome-wide, all along the nuclear radius, which we name genomic loci positioning by sequencing or GPSeq. Using GPSeq, we generate the first high-resolution map of radial chromatin organization in human cells, which reveals a clear tendency of individual genomic regions to occupy specific radial locations, as well as gradients of chromatin modifications, transcriptional activity and replication timing, and a marked polar arrangement of chromosomes with respect to A/B compartments and subcompartments38,39. We develop a high-performance algorithm, chromflock, that dramatically improves the accuracy of whole-genome structure ensemble generation. Finally, we integrate GPSeq maps with DNA breaks and mutations data, revealing radial differences in DNA damage and mutational processes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":355,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":1585,"length":10},{"style":"sup","offset":124,"length":5},{"style":"sup","offset":141,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":174,"length":8},{"style":"sup","offset":365,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":541,"length":7},{"style":"sup","offset":1537,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eqfnm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Note: a separate set of samples (coverslips) should be prepared for visualization of gradual genome restriction by YFISH and for sequencing. The two sets of coverslips should be processed in parallel.
Wash the cells with 1X PBS containing Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ pre-warmed to 37 °C
Incubate for 2 min at room temperature (RT)
Aspirate the PBS solution from the dish
Add 0.4X PBS with Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ at RT
Incubate for 1 min at RT
Aspirate the PBS solution
Add 0.4X PBS/4% PFA at RT
Incubate for 10 min at RT
Aspirate the fixative solution from the dish
Quench the residual PFA by adding 1X PBS/125 mM glycine
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS at RT
Wash the cells in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 three times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Aspirate the PBS solution and then add 1X PBS/0.5% Triton X-100 at RT
Incubate for 20 min at RT
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS at RT
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS/20% glycerol at RT
Incubate the samples in 1X PBS/20% glycerol for 18 h at RT
Fill a cryo-resistant Dewar flask with liquid N 2
Remove the glycerol solution from the dish (leave a thin layer on top of the cells)
Place the dish on the surface of liquid N 2 for 20–30 sec
Place the dish at RT and let the samples thaw gradually, making sure that they do not dry
Once all the samples are thawed, fill the dish with fresh 1X PBS/20% glycerol at RT
Incubate for 3 min at RT
Repeat steps 20–23 four times
Wash the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 two times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Briefly rinse the samples in 0.1 M HCl at RT
Incubate the samples in 0.1 M HCl for 5 min at RT Note: the samples should be kept in HCl for a maximum of 5 min including the quick rinsing step below
Briefly rinse the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 at RT
Wash the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 two times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Wash the samples for 5 min in 2X SSC
Replace the solution to fresh 2X SSC and process the samples immediately or alternatively store them in 2X SSC/0.05% NaN 3 for up to 1 month at 4 °C

Place coverslips in a 10 cm dish, cover them with 5 ml of the PLL solution, and then gently shake the dishes for 15 min at RT AT WHICH TEMPERATURE? Note: make sure that coverslips stay covered by liquid
Aspirate the solution and transfer it into a 15 ml tube Note: the solution can be stored at 4 °C and used up to three times (STORED AT WHICH TEMPERATURE?)
Wash the coverslips three times with 1X PBS at RT and once with ethanol (EtOH) 70%
Air-dry the coverslips and proceed with cell spotting and fixation Breakpoint: if not used immediately, coverslips can be stored in EtOH 70% at 4 °C and air-dried just before use.
Place the desired number of PLL-coated coverslips into a 6-well cell culture plate
In each well, dispense a cell suspension freshly prepared in 1X PBS with Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ Note: we usually spot up to 5×10 5 cells onto a 22x22 mm coverslip
Let the cells sediment onto the coverslip for 10 min at RT
Slowly add 0.4X PBS with Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ at RT
Incubate for 1 min at RT
Aspirate the PBS solution
Slowly add 0.4X PBS/4% PFA at RT
Incubate for 10 min at RT
Aspirate the fixative solution from the dish
Quench the residual PFA by adding 1X PBS/125 mM glycine
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS at RT
Wash the cells in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 three times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Aspirate the PBS solution and then add 1X PBS/0.5% Triton X-100 at RT
Incubate for 20 min at RT
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS at RT
Briefly wash the cells in 1X PBS/20% glycerol at RT
Incubate the samples in 1X PBS/20% glycerol for 18 h at RT
Fill a cryo-resistant Dewar flask with liquid N 2
Remove the glycerol solution from the dish (leave a thin layer on top of the cells)
Place the dish on the surface of liquid N 2 for 20–30 sec
Place the dish at RT and let the samples thaw gradually, making sure that they do not dry
Once all the samples are thawed, fill the dish with fresh 1X PBS/20% glycerol at RT
Incubate for 3 min at RT
Repeat steps 20–23 four times
Wash the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 two times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Briefly rinse the samples in 0.1 M HCl at RT
Incubate the samples in 0.1 M HCl for 5 min at RT Note: the samples should be kept in HCl for a maximum of 5 min including the quick rinsing step below
Briefly rinse the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 at RT
Wash the samples in 1X PBS/0.05% Triton X-100 two times, 5 min each at RT, while shaking
Wash the samples for 5 min in 2X SSC
Replace the solution to fresh 2X SSC and process the samples immediately or alternatively store them in 2X SSC/0.05% NaN 3 for up to 1 month at 4 °C
Bring the 2X SSC/50 mM phosphate buffer/50% FA (FPS) solution to RT
Rinse the samples with FPS at RT
Exchange to fresh FPS and store at RT protected from light for 20 h
Wash the samples in 2X SSC for 5 min at RT
Wash the samples in 1X CutSmart, two times, 5 min each at RT, while gently rocking

Prepare the following digestion mix (volumes for one 22x22 mm coverslip): · CutSmart (10X) 40 μl · HindIII-HF (20,000 U/ml) 10 μl · Nuclease-free water Up to 400 μl Alternatively: · CutSmart (10X) 40 μl · MboI (25,000 U/ml) 8 μl · Nuclease-free water Up to 400 μl Note: The choice of enzyme depends on the desired resolution and affordable sequencing depth. As a rule of thumb, HindIII can be used to obtain highly reproducible radiality maps at 1 Mb resolution using five different digestion durations and one sequencing run on NextSeq 500. MboI is preferable when a higher resolution is needed.
Pre-warm the digestion mix for at least 10 min at 37 °C

Dispense the digestion mix on a piece of Parafilm placed inside a humidity chamber Note: we make the chamber by covering the edges of a 15 cm dish with tissue paper pre-soaked in distilled water, and then laying a piece of parafilm on the dish bottom
Gently flip one sample at a time, onto a droplet of the digestion mix

Incubate each coverslip at 37 °C in the sealed humidity chamber, for a selected period of time Note: each sample is incubated in the presence of the restriction enzyme for a different duration. HAP1 cells : for HindIII, we have used 10, 15, 30, 45 min, 1, 2, and 6 h, whereas, for MboI, we have used 1, 5, 10, 15, and 30 min, which were sufficient to produce reproducible radiality maps. GM06990 cells : for HindIII, we have used 10, 15, 30 min, and 2 h. For different cell types, it might be necessary to optimize the number and series of incubation times, by first monitoring the enzyme diffusion by YFISH (see below)
Stop the digestion by washing the samples with ice-cold 1X PBS/50 mM EDTA/0.01% Triton X-100 three times, 5 min each, on ice
Rinse the samples with 1X CutSmart/0.01 % Triton X-100 at RT
Wash with 1X CutSmart/0.01 % Triton X-100 for 5 min at RT
Wash the samples in 1X Alkaline phosphatase (AP) buffer for 5 min at RT
Prepare the following dephosphorylation mix (volumes for one 22x22 mm coverslip): · AP buffer (10X) 40 μl · AP (1 U/μl) 6 μl · Nuclease-free water 354 μl
Dispense the dephosphorylation mix on a piece of Parafilm placed inside a humidity chamber
Gently flip one coverslip at a time, onto a droplet of the dephosphorylation mix
Incubate the samples for 2 h at 37 °C in the sealed humidity chamber
Transfer the samples back to a 6-well plate
Wash the samples with 1X CutSmart/0.01 % Triton X-100 two times, 5 min each at RT
Wash the samples in 1X T4 Ligase buffer two times, 5 min each at RT, while gently rocking

Prepare the following ligation mix I (volumes for one 22x22 mm coverslip): · Ligase buffer (10X) 21 μl · ATP (10 mM) 24 μl · BSA (5 mg/ml) 6 μl · HindIII GPSeq adapter (10 µM) 6 μl · Alternatively: MboI GPSeq adapter (10 µM) 18 μl · Nuclease-free water Up to 210 μl Note: thaw all reagents slowly on ice or at 4 °C
Dispense the ligation mix I on a piece of Parafilm placed inside a humidity chamber
Gently flip one sample at a time, onto the droplet of ligation mix I
Incubate the samples for 30 min at RT
Prepare the following ligation mix II (volumes for one 22x22 mm coverslip): · Nuclease-free water 45 μl · Ligase buffer (10X) 9 μl · T4 Ligase enzyme 36 μl
Gently pipette the ligase mix II under each coverslip in and out 7–8 times, making sure not to make bubbles
Incubate the samples for 18 h at 16 °C in the sealed humidity chamber
In a new 6-well plate, wash the samples in 1X HSW five times, 1 hour each at 37 °C, while shaking

Wash the samples in 1X PBS three times, 5 min each at RT Breakpoint: at this point, the samples for sequencing can be stored in 1X PBS at 4 °C for several days until the results of YFISH (next section) are available
Note: we recommend to check that the genome has been gradually digested by the enzyme, from the nuclear periphery towards the nucleus interior, before proceeding to sequencing
Equilibrate the samples in 2X SSC/25% FA
Prepare the following hybridization mix (volumes for one 22x22 mm coverslip): · YHB 297 μl · Fluorescently labelled oligonucleotide (2 μM) 3 μl
Dispense the hybridization mix on a piece of Parafilm placed inside a humidity chamber
Gently flip one sample at a time, onto the droplet of hybridization mix

Incubate the samples for 18 h at 30 °C inside the sealed humidity chamber Note: the samples are now light sensitive, therefore all the subsequent steps should be performed by protecting them from light
Transfer the samples into a 6-well plate pre-filled with 2 ml/well of YWB pre-warmed at RT
Exchange the YWB solution and incubate for 1 h at 30 °C, while gently shaking
Remove the YWB solution and add 2 ml/well of YWB/0.1 ng/ml Hoechst 33342
Incubate for 30 min at 30 °C, while gently shaking
Quickly rinse the samples with 2X SSC
Mount the samples and image them using wield-field microscopy or, if available, STED microscopy
Note: for each of the restriction times used, an individual sequencing library is prepared
Wash the samples with nuclease-free water three times, 2 min each at RT
Scrape each coverslip in the following DNA extraction mix: · DEB 100 μl · Proteinase K (0.8 U/μl) 10 μl
Transfer each sample into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube
Incubate the samples for 18 h at 56 °C, while shaking at 800 rpm
Inactivate the enzyme by incubating for 10 min at 96 °C
Place the samples on ice
To each sample, add an equal volume of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol 25:24:1, then shake the samples vigorously
Centrifuge the samples at 20,000 x g for 5 min at RT
Collect the upper phase and transfer it to a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube
Add an equal volume of chloroform to it, and shake vigorously
Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 5 min at RT
Collect the upper phase and transfer it to a new 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube, then add sodium acetate to a final concentration of 0.3 M
Add 3.7 μl of glycogen per each 100 μl of the solution
Add 2.5X volumes of absolute ethanol at –20 °C and incubate the samples for 18 h at –80 °C
Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 90 min at 4 °C
Discard the liquid and wash the pellet with 600 μl of ice-cold 70% ethanol
Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 15 min at 4 °C
Discard the liquid and wash the pellet with 600 μl of ice-cold 70% ethanol
Centrifuge at 20,000 x g for 15 min at 4 °C
Discard the supernatant and dry the pellet Note: do not over dry the pellet, as this might result in low DNA recovery
Dissolve the pellet in 100 μl of TE buffer Breakpoint: at this point, the samples can be stored a –20 °C until sonication

Sonicate the samples aiming to achieve a mean DNA fragment size of 300–500 base pairs (bp). We routinely use Bioruptor® Plus with the following settings: 30 sec ON, 90 sec OFF, high mode, 16 cycles. Alternatively, a Covaris S-series sonicator can be used with the following settings: duty 10%, intensity 4, time 30 sec, cycle/burst 200, 4 cycles in 50 μl TE/water.
Pre-warm AMPure XP beads for 30 min at RT
To each sample, add 80 μl of AMPure XP bead suspension
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up-down 5–6 times
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Place the samples on a magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet and the liquid is completely transparent
Carefully aspirate and discard the supernatant
Add 200 μl per sample of ice-cold 80% ethanol, while the samples are attached to the magnetic stand
Aspirate the supernatant
Repeat the wash in ice-cold 80% ethanol once
Air-dry the beads for 5 min at RT Note: do not over-dry the beads, as this might result in low DNA recovery
Remove the samples from the magnetic stand
Resuspend each sample in 10 μl of nuclease-free water
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Place the samples on the magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet
Transfer 7.5 μl of the cleared solution into a 0.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube

Measure DNA concentration using the Qubit® dsDNA HS Assay Kit Checkpoint: check the DNA fragment size distribution by loading 1 μl of sonicated DNA into an High Sensitivity DNA Kit, and run it on Bioanalyzer 2100. Breakpoint: at this point, the samples can be stored at –20 °C until in vitro transcription

In a 0.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube prepare the following IVT mix: · Sonicated DNA 7.5 μl · rNTPs mix* 8 μl · T7 polymerase buffer 10X* 2 μl · T7 polymerase* 2 μl · RiboSafe RNase Inhibitor 0.5 μl Note: For HAP1 samples digested with HindIII, we typically use 300 ng of sonicated DNA as input to the IVT reaction, whereas for those digested with MboI we use 50 ng. For GM06990 samples digested with HindIII, we use 133 ng of sonicated DNA Note: all the reagents marked by * are included in the MEGAscript® kit. The rNTPs mix is prepared by mixing equal volumes of each rNTP solution included in the MEGAscript® kit
Incubate the samples for 14 h at 37 °C in a thermocycler with the lid set at 70 °C
To each sample, add 2U (1 μl) of DNase I
Incubate the samples for 15 min at 37 °C. Meanwhile, prewarm the Agencourt RNAClean XP beads for 30 min at RT
Transfer each IVT product into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube
Bring the volume of each sample up to 30 μl with nuclease-free water
Add a 1.8 vol./vol. ratio of RNAClean XP beads suspension to the IVT product
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up-down 5-6 times
Incubate for 10 min at RT
Place the samples on a magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet and the liquid is completely transparent
Aspirate and discard the supernatant
Add 200 μl per sample of ice-cold 80% ethanol, while the samples are attached to the magnetic stand
Incubate for 1–2 min at RT
Aspirate the supernatant
Repeat the wash with ethanol 80% two times more
Airdry the beads for 10 min at RT Note: do not over-dry the beads, as this might result in low DNA recovery
Remove the samples from the magnetic stand
Resuspend the beads in 7.5 μl of nuclease-free water
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Place the sample on the magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet

Transfer 6 μl of each sample into a separate 0.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube Checkpoint: check the RNA fragment size distribution by loading 1 μl of purified IVT product into an RNA 6000 Pico Kit, and run it on a Bioanalyzer 2100
Note: all the subsequent steps are done on ice, unless otherwise specified
To each sample, add 1 μl of RA3 adapter
In a thermocycler, incubate for 2 min at 70 °C with the lid set at 70 °C
Immediately place the samples on ice
To each sample, add 3 μl of the following RNA ligase mix: · T4 RNA Ligase buffer 10X 1 μl · T4 RNA Ligase 2, truncated (200 U/μl) 1 μl · RNaseOUT™ 1 μl
Incubate the samples for 2 h at 25 °C in a thermocycler with the lid set at 30 °C
Transfer the samples onto ice
Note: all the subsequent steps are done on ice, unless otherwise specified
To each sample, add 3.5 μl of RTP (10 μM) primer
Incubate the samples for 2 min at 70 °C in a thermocycler with the lid set at 70 °C
Immediately place the samples on ice

Add 11.5 μl per sample of reverse transcription mix: · 1st strand buffer* 5 μl · dNTPs mix 0.5 μl · DTT 100 mM* 2 μl · SuperScript® IV* 2 μl · RNaseOUT™ 2 μl Note: all the reagents marked by * are included in the SuperScript® IV Reverse Transcriptase kit
Incubate the samples for 1 h at 50 °C in a thermocycler Breakpoint: at this point, the samples can be stored at –20 °C
Transfer each sample into a 200 μl PCR tube
To each sample, add 4 μl of the desired RPI (10 μM) primer
To each sample, add 71 μl of PCR mix: · NEBNext® 2X PCR Mix 50 μl · RP1 primer 4 μl · Nuclease-free water 17 μl

Perform the following cycles in a thermocycler: A. 98 °C, 30 sec B. 98 °C, 10 sec C. 60 °C, 30 sec D. 72 °C, 30 sec E. Go to B and repeat for 8–14 cycles F. 72 °C, 10 min G. 4 °C, hold Note: the number of PCR cycles should be adjusted based on the amount of genomic DNA used in the IVT reaction and the restriction enzyme used. For example, for a 22x22 mm coverslip fully covered with HAP1 cells digested with HindIII, we typically use 9 PCR cycles.
Prewarm the AMPure XP beads for 30 min at RT
Transfer each PCR product into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube
Add a 0.8 vol./vol. ratio of AMPure XP bead suspension to each sample
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up-down 5-6 times
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Place the samples on a magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet and the liquid is completely transparent
Aspirate and discard the supernatant
Add 200 μl per sample of ice-cold 80% ethanol, while the samples are attached to the magnetic stand
Aspirate the supernatant
Repeat once the wash in ice-cold 80% ethanol
Airdry the beads for 5 min at RT Note: do not over-dry the beads, as this might result in low DNA recovery
Remove the samples from the magnetic stand
Resuspend each sample in 20 μl of nuclease-free water
Incubate for 5 min at RT
Place the samples on the magnetic stand
Incubate for 5 min until all the beads have attached to the magnet
For each sample separately, transfer 17–18 μl of the cleared solution into a 1.5 ml Eppendorf® DNA LoBind tube
Measure DNA concentration using the Qubit® dsDNA HS Assay Kit

Store the libraries at –20 °C Checkpoint: 1 μl of library can be checked on a Bioanalyzer 2100 using a High Sensitivity DNA Kit Note: at this point, the libraries can be stored at –20 °C up to one year
